Chapter-X - Fair Practices Code
A.
Fair Practice Code
A.1 Application of the Code

children.title: 129. This Code shall apply to all the products and services, whether they are provided
children.text:
129. This Code shall apply to all the products and services, whether they are provided
by the HFC, its subsidiaries or Digital Lending Platforms (self-owned and/or under an
outsourcing arrangement) across the counter, over the phone, by post, through interactive
electronic devices, on the internet or by any other method.

children.title: Note: In case of outsourced lending platforms, the HFC must meticulously follow
children.text:
Note: In case of outsourced lending platforms, the HFC must meticulously follow
regulatory instructions on outsourcing of financial services and IT services.
A.2 Applications for loans and their processing

children.title: 130. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraphs 10 to 12 of Reserve Bank of
children.text:
130. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraphs 10 to 12 of Reserve Bank of
India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions,
2025.

children.title: 131. The HFC shall transparently disclose to the borrower all the following information:
children.text:
131. The HFC shall transparently disclose to the borrower all the following information:
(1) fees/ charges payable for processing the loan application;
(2) the amount of fees refundable if loan amount is not sanctioned/ disbursed,
(3) pre-payment options and charges, if any,
(4) penal charges for delayed repayment, if any,
(5) conversion charges for switching loan from fixed to floating rates or vice-versa,
(6) existence of any interest reset clause and any other matter which affects the
interest of the borrower.

children.title: 132. In other words, the HFC must disclose 'all in cost' inclusive of all charges involved
children.text:
132. In other words, the HFC must disclose 'all in cost' inclusive of all charges involved
in processing/ sanctioning of loan application in a transparent manner. It should also be
ensured that such charges/ fees are non-discriminatory.
A.3 Loan appraisal, terms / conditions and communication of rejection of loan
application

children.title: 133. Normally all particulars required for processing the loan application shall be
children.text:
133. Normally all particulars required for processing the loan application shall be
collected by the HFC at the time of application. In case it needs any additional information,
the customer should be told immediately that he would be contacted again.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 134. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraphs 13 and 14 of Reserve Bank
children.text:
134. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraphs 13 and 14 of Reserve Bank
of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions,
2025.

children.title: 135. If the HFC cannot provide the loan to the customer, it shall communicate in writing
children.text:
135. If the HFC cannot provide the loan to the customer, it shall communicate in writing
the reason(s) for rejection.
A.4 Disbursement of loans including changes in terms and conditions

children.title: 136. Disbursement should be made in accordance with the disbursement schedule
children.text:
136. Disbursement should be made in accordance with the disbursement schedule
given in the Loan Agreement/ Sanction Letter.

children.title: 137. HFCs shall give notice to the borrower of any change in the terms and conditions
children.text:
137. HFCs shall give notice to the borrower of any change in the terms and conditions
as prescribed under paragraph 15 of Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial
Companies - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025. If such change is to the
disadvantage of the customer, he/ she may within 60 days and without notice close his/
her account or switch it without having to pay any extra charges or interest

children.title: 138. HFCs shall also comply with the provisions of paragraphs 16 and 17 of Reserve
children.text:
138. HFCs shall also comply with the provisions of paragraphs 16 and 17 of Reserve
Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Responsible Business Conduct)
Directions, 2025.
A.5 Responsibility of Board of Directors

children.title: 139. The Board of Directors of HFCs should lay down the appropriate grievance
children.text:
139. The Board of Directors of HFCs should lay down the appropriate grievance
redressal mechanism within the organization to resolve complaints and grievances. Such
a mechanism should ensure that all disputes arising out of the decisions of lending
institution's functionaries are heard and disposed of at least at the next higher level.

children.title: 140. The Board of Directors of each HFC should provide for periodical review of the
children.text:
140. The Board of Directors of each HFC should provide for periodical review of the
compliance of the Fair Practices Code and the functioning of the grievances redressal
mechanism at various levels of management. A consolidated report of such reviews may
be submitted to the Board at regular intervals, as may be prescribed by it.
A.6 Complaints and Grievance Redressal

children.title: 141. The HFC shall have a system and a procedure for receiving, registering and
children.text:
141. The HFC shall have a system and a procedure for receiving, registering and
disposing of complaints and grievances in each of its offices, including those received on-
line.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 142. If a complaint has been received in writing from a customer, the HFC shall
children.text:
142. If a complaint has been received in writing from a customer, the HFC shall
endeavour to send him/ her an acknowledgement/ response within a week. The
acknowledgement should contain the name & designation of the official who will deal with
the grievance. If the complaint is relayed over phone at the HFC's designated telephone
helpdesk or customer service number, the customer shall be provided with a complaint
reference number and be kept informed of the progress within a reasonable period of
time.

children.title: 143. After examining the matter, the HFC shall send the customer its final response or
children.text:
143. After examining the matter, the HFC shall send the customer its final response or
explain why it needs more time to respond and shall endeavour to do so within six weeks
of receipt of a complaint and he/ she should be informed how to take his/ her complaint
further if he/ she is still not satisfied.

children.title: 144. The HFC shall publicise its grievance redressal procedure (e-mail id and other
children.text:
144. The HFC shall publicise its grievance redressal procedure (e-mail id and other
contact details at which the complaints can be lodged, turnaround time for resolving the
issue, matrix for escalation, etc.) for lodging the complaints by the aggrieved borrower
and ensure specifically that it is made available on its website. The HFC shall clearly
display in all its offices/ branches and on the website that in case the complainant does
not receive response from the company within a period of one month or is dissatisfied
with the response received, the complainant may approach the Complaint Redressal Cell
of National Housing Bank (NHB) by lodging its complaints online on the website of NHB
or through post to NHB, New Delhi.
A.7 Language and mode of communicating Fair Practice Code

children.title: 145. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraph 21 of Reserve Bank of India
children.text:
145. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraph 21 of Reserve Bank of India
(Non-Banking Financial Companies - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025.
A.8 Regulation of excessive interest charged by HFCs

children.title: 146. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraphs 22 to 24 of Reserve Bank of
children.text:
146. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraphs 22 to 24 of Reserve Bank of
India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions,
2025.

children.title: 147. Instalments collected from borrowers should clearly indicate the bifurcation
children.text:
147. Instalments collected from borrowers should clearly indicate the bifurcation
between interest and principal.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
A.9 Excessive interest charged by HFCs

children.title: 148. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraph 25 of Reserve Bank of India
children.text:
148. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraph 25 of Reserve Bank of India
(Non-Banking Financial Companies - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025.

children.title: 149. HFCs shall lay out appropriate internal principles and procedures in determining
children.text:
149. HFCs shall lay out appropriate internal principles and procedures in determining
interest rates and processing and other charges (including penal charges, if any). In this
regard the directions in the Fair Practices Code about transparency in respect of terms
and conditions of the loans are to be kept in view. HFCs shall also put in place an internal
mechanism to monitor the process and the operations so as to ensure adequate
transparency in communications with the borrowers.
A.10 Advertising, Marketing and Sales

children.title: 150. The HFC shall ensure that all advertising and promotional material is clear, and
children.text:
150. The HFC shall ensure that all advertising and promotional material is clear, and
factual.

children.title: 151. In any advertising in any media and promotional literature that draws attention to
children.text:
151. In any advertising in any media and promotional literature that draws attention to
a service or product and includes a reference to an interest rate, the HFC shall also
indicate whether other fees and charges will apply and that full details of the relevant
terms and conditions are available on request or on the website.

children.title: 152. The HFC shall provide information on interest rates, common fees and charges
children.text:
152. The HFC shall provide information on interest rates, common fees and charges
(including penal charges, if any) through putting up notices in its branches; through
telephone or help-lines; on the company's website; through designated staff/ help desk;
or providing service guide / tariff schedule.

children.title: 153. If the HFC avails of the services of third parties for providing support services, the
children.text:
153. If the HFC avails of the services of third parties for providing support services, the
HFC shall require that such third parties handle customer's personal information (if any
available to such third parties) with the same degree of confidentiality and security as the
HFC would.

children.title: 154. The HFC may, from time to time, communicate to customers various features of its
children.text:
154. The HFC may, from time to time, communicate to customers various features of its
products availed by them. Information about its other products or promotional offers in
respect of products/ services, shall be conveyed to customers only if they have given their
consent to receive such information/ service either by mail or by registering for the same
on the website or on customer service number.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
A.11 Code of Conduct for DSAs/DMAs

children.title: 155. The HFC shall prescribe a code of conduct for its Direct Selling Agencies (DSAs)
children.text:
155. The HFC shall prescribe a code of conduct for its Direct Selling Agencies (DSAs)
/ Direct Marketing Agents (DMAs) whose services are availed to market products /
services which amongst other matters require them to identify themselves when they
approach the customer for selling products personally or through phone.

children.title: 156. The HFC shall adopt the following Model Code of Conducts for DSAs/ DMAs :
children.text:
156. The HFC shall adopt the following Model Code of Conducts for DSAs/ DMAs :
(1) Model Code of Conduct for DSAs / DMAs is a code of conduct for adoption by HFCs
in respect of DSAs/ DMAs operating as their Agents. The Code is a set of guidelines
designed to ensure that DSAs/ DMAs of HFC act and conduct in conformity with the
laid down policies and procedures as set in the Code.
(2) Applicability

children.title: (i) Upon adoption and inclusion as part of agreement between HFC and the DSA /
children.text:
(i) Upon adoption and inclusion as part of agreement between HFC and the DSA /
DMA, this Code will apply to person/ legal entity involved in marketing and
distribution of any loan or other financial products or services of HFC.

children.title: (ii) The DSA / DMA or / and its employees / representatives must agree to abide by this
children.text:
(ii) The DSA / DMA or / and its employees / representatives must agree to abide by this
Code prior to undertaking any direct marketing operation and distribution on behalf
of the HFC.

children.title: (iii) Any employee / representative of DSA / DMA found to be violating this Code may
children.text:
(iii) Any employee / representative of DSA / DMA found to be violating this Code may
be blacklisted and such action taken be reported to the HFC from time to time by
the DSA / DMA.

children.title: (iv) Failure to comply with this requirement may result in permanent termination of
children.text:
(iv) Failure to comply with this requirement may result in permanent termination of
business of DSA / DMA with HFC and may even lead to permanent blacklisting.

children.title: (v) A declaration-cum-undertaking to be given by DSA / DMA to HFC and be obtained
children.text:
(v) A declaration-cum-undertaking to be given by DSA / DMA to HFC and be obtained
from its employees / representatives by the DSAs / DMAs before assigning them
duties is annexed as Annex III.
(3) Tele-calling a Prospect (a prospective customer)

children.title: (i) With reference to Unsolicited Commercial Communications - National Do Not Call
children.text:
(i) With reference to Unsolicited Commercial Communications - National Do Not Call
Registry (NCND), the HFC shall comply with the provisions of paragraph 101 of
Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Responsible Business
Conduct) Directions, 2025.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (ii) A prospect is to be contacted for sourcing an HFC's product / service or HFC related
children.text:
(ii) A prospect is to be contacted for sourcing an HFC's product / service or HFC related
product / service only under the following circumstances:

children.title: (a) When a prospect has expressed desire to acquire any loan or other financial
children.text:
(a) When a prospect has expressed desire to acquire any loan or other financial
product or services through the HFC' internet site/ digital platforms including
mobile applications/ call centre/ branch or through the Relationship Manager at
the HFC or has been referred to by another prospect/ customer or is an existing
customer of the HFC who has given explicit consent in writing/ digitally for
accepting calls on other products/ services of the HFC.

children.title: (b) When the prospect's name/ telephone number/ address is available and obtained
children.text:
(b) When the prospect's name/ telephone number/ address is available and obtained
after taking his/ her explicit consent in writing/ digitally on a separate document.

children.title: (iii) DSA / DMA or / and its employees / representatives should not call a person whose
children.text:
(iii) DSA / DMA or / and its employees / representatives should not call a person whose
name / number is flagged in any "Do Not Disturb" list.
(4) Timing to contact a prospect on telephone

children.title: (i) DSA/ DMAs must introduce themselves and before calling must share their contact
children.text:
(i) DSA/ DMAs must introduce themselves and before calling must share their contact
details through message or any other written mode including his/ her name, contact
number, DMA/ DSA they are employed with and the HFC they are representing.

children.title: (ii) Telephonic contact must normally be between 09:30 hours and 19:00 hours.
(iii) Calls earlier or later than the prescribed time period may be placed only when the
(iv) Residence/ Business/ Office address visit must normally be limited between 09:30
children.text:
(iv) Residence/ Business/ Office address visit must normally be limited between 09:30
hours and 19:00 hours. Visit earlier or later than the prescribed time period may be
made only when prospect has expressly authorized DSA or/and its employees/
representatives to do so either in writing or orally.
(5) DSA/ DMA or/and its employees/ representatives should respect a prospect's privacy
and his/ her interest may normally be discussed only with the prospect and with any
other individual/ family member such as prospect's accountant/ secretary/ spouse only
when authorized to do so by the prospect.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(6) For leaving messages and contacting persons other than the prospect, calls must first
be placed to the prospect. If the prospect is not available, a message may be left for
him / her. The aim of the message should be to get the prospect to return the call or
to check for a convenient time to call again. Ordinarily, such messages may be
restricted to:
"Please leave a message that XXXXXX (name of officer) representing YYYYYY
(name of the HFC) called and requested to call back at ZZZZZZ (phone number)". As
a general rule, the message must indicate that the purpose of the call is regarding
selling or distributing a product of an HFC.
(7) DSA/ DMA or/and its employees/ representatives shall not:

children.title: (i) Mislead the prospect on any product/ service offered by an HFC;
children.text: (i) Mislead the prospect on any product/ service offered by an HFC;

children.title: (ii) Mislead the prospect about their business or organization's name or falsely
children.text:
(ii) Mislead the prospect about their business or organization's name or falsely
represent themselves as HFC's employee;

children.title: (iii) Make any false/ unauthorized commitment on behalf of an HFC for any facility/ loan/
children.text:
(iii) Make any false/ unauthorized commitment on behalf of an HFC for any facility/ loan/
service.
(8) Telemarketing Etiquettes

children.title: (i) Pre Call
children.text: (i) Pre Call

children.title: (a) No calls prior to 09:30 hours or post 19:00 hours unless specifically requested;
children.text: (a) No calls prior to 09:30 hours or post 19:00 hours unless specifically requested;

children.title: (b) No serial calling;
children.text: (b) No serial calling;

children.title: (c) No calling on lists unless list is cleared by the DSA/DMA leader.
children.text: (c) No calling on lists unless list is cleared by the DSA/DMA leader.

children.title: (ii) During Call
children.text: (ii) During Call

children.title: (a) Identify yourself, your company and your principal;
children.text: (a) Identify yourself, your company and your principal;

children.title: (b) Request permission to proceed;
children.text: (b) Request permission to proceed;

children.title: (c) If denied permission, apologize and politely disconnect;
children.text: (c) If denied permission, apologize and politely disconnect;

children.title: (d) State reason for your call;
children.text: (d) State reason for your call;

children.title: (e) Always offer to call back on landline, if call is made to a cell number;
children.text: (e) Always offer to call back on landline, if call is made to a cell number;

children.title: (f) Never interrupt or argue;
children.text: (f) Never interrupt or argue;

children.title: (g) To the extent possible, talk in the language which is most comfortable to the
children.text:
(g) To the extent possible, talk in the language which is most comfortable to the
prospect/ customer;

children.title: (h) Keep the conversation limited to business matters;
children.text: (h) Keep the conversation limited to business matters;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (i) Check for understanding of "Most Important Terms and Conditions" by the
children.text:
(i) Check for understanding of "Most Important Terms and Conditions" by the
prospect/ customer if he plans to buy the product;

children.title: (j) Reconfirm next call or next visit details;
children.text: (j) Reconfirm next call or next visit details;

children.title: (k) Provide your telephone no, your supervisor's name or the HFC's officer contact
children.text:
(k) Provide your telephone no, your supervisor's name or the HFC's officer contact
details if asked for by the prospect/ customer;

children.title: (l) Thank the prospect/customer for his/ her time.
children.text: (l) Thank the prospect/customer for his/ her time.

children.title: (iii) Post Call
children.text: (iii) Post Call

children.title: (a) Prospects/ Customers who have expressed their lack of interest for the offering
children.text:
(a) Prospects/ Customers who have expressed their lack of interest for the offering
should not be called for the next 3 months with the same offer;

children.title: (b) Provide feedback to the HFC on prospects/ customers who have expressed their
children.text:
(b) Provide feedback to the HFC on prospects/ customers who have expressed their
desire to be flagged "Do Not Disturb";

children.title: (c) Never call or entertain calls from customers regarding products already sold;
children.text: (c) Never call or entertain calls from customers regarding products already sold;

children.title: (d) Advise them to contact the Customer Service Staff of HFC.
children.text:
(d) Advise them to contact the Customer Service Staff of HFC.
(9) DSA/ DMA or/and its employees/ representatives will

children.title: (i) not accept gifts or bribes of any kind from prospects/ customers. Further, if he/ she
children.text:
(i) not accept gifts or bribes of any kind from prospects/ customers. Further, if he/ she
is offered a bribe or payment of any kind by the prospect/ customer, it must be
reported to his/ her management.

children.title: (ii) not offer any gifts/ gratitude in cash or in kind to the prospect/ customer to solicit
children.text:
(ii) not offer any gifts/ gratitude in cash or in kind to the prospect/ customer to solicit
business.
(10) DSA/ DMA or/and its employees/ representatives should:

children.title: (i) respect personal space, maintain adequate distance from the prospect/ customer;
children.text: (i) respect personal space, maintain adequate distance from the prospect/ customer;

children.title: (ii) ensure that prospect/ customer is not visited within a period of 3 months of
children.text:
(ii) ensure that prospect/ customer is not visited within a period of 3 months of
expression of lack of interest for the offering by him/ her.

children.title: (iii) not enter the prospect's/ customer's residence/ office against his/ her wishes;
children.text: (iii) not enter the prospect's/ customer's residence/ office against his/ her wishes;

children.title: (iv) prospect's/ customer's residence/ business is visited by not more than one
children.text:
(iv) prospect's/ customer's residence/ business is visited by not more than one
employee/ representative of DSA/ DMA and one supervisor, if required;

children.title: (v) respect the prospect's privacy;
children.text: (v) respect the prospect's privacy;

children.title: (vi) end the visit with a request for the prospect to call back, if the prospect/ customer
<image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (vii) provide his/ her telephone number, name of the supervisor or the concerned HFC
children.text:
(vii) provide his/ her telephone number, name of the supervisor or the concerned HFC
officer's contact details, if asked for, by the prospect/ customer; and

children.title: (viii) limit discussions to prospects of the business and maintain a professional distance.
children.text:
(viii) limit discussions to prospects of the business and maintain a professional distance.
(11) DSA/ DMA or/and its employees/ representatives must be in proper formal attire
while meeting up with prospect/ customer.
(12) Any communication sent to the prospect shall only be in the mode and format
approved by the HFC.
(13) While there is no specific qualification requirement for individuals, corporate
entities depending upon the nature of the entity, shall ensure that the Partnership
Deed, Memorandum of Association or any other document evidencing the constitution
of the entity shall contain as one of its main objects soliciting or procuring DSA
business.
(14) DSA/ DMA seeking of engagement/ empanelment with the HFC shall submit the
application for empanelment in the illustrative format given at Annex III.
(15) Outsourcing Agreement

children.title: (i) The terms and conditions governing the contract between the HFC and the service
children.text:
(i) The terms and conditions governing the contract between the HFC and the service
provider should be carefully defined in written agreements and vetted by HFC's legal
counsel on their legal effect and enforceability.

children.title: (ii) Every such agreement should address the risks and risk mitigation strategies. The
children.text:
(ii) Every such agreement should address the risks and risk mitigation strategies. The
agreement should be sufficiently flexible to allow the HFC to retain an appropriate
level of control over the outsourcing and the right to intervene with appropriate
measures to meet legal and regulatory obligations.

children.title: (iii) The agreement should also bring out the nature of legal relationship between the
children.text:
(iii) The agreement should also bring out the nature of legal relationship between the
parties - i.e. whether the agent, principal or otherwise. Some of the key provisions
of the contract should be the following:

children.title: (a) The contract should clearly define what activities are going to be outsourced
children.text:
(a) The contract should clearly define what activities are going to be outsourced
including appropriate service and performance standards;

children.title: (b) The HFC must ensure it has the ability to access all books, records and
children.text:
(b) The HFC must ensure it has the ability to access all books, records and
information relevant to the outsourced activity available with the service provider;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (c) The contract should provide for continuous monitoring and assessment by the
(d) A termination clause and minimum period to execute a termination provision, if
children.text:
(d) A termination clause and minimum period to execute a termination provision, if
deemed necessary, should be included;

children.title: (e) Controls to ensure customer data confidentiality and service providers' liability in
(f) There must be contingency plans to ensure business continuity;
children.text: (f) There must be contingency plans to ensure business continuity;

children.title: (g) The contract should provide for the prohibition of further outsourcing by the
children.text:
(g) The contract should provide for the prohibition of further outsourcing by the
service provider for all or part of an outsourced activity;

children.title: (h) It should provide the HFC with the right to conduct audits on the service provider
children.text:
(h) It should provide the HFC with the right to conduct audits on the service provider
whether by its internal or external auditors, or by agents appointed to act on its
behalf and to obtain copies of any audit or review reports and findings made on
the service provider in conjunction with the services performed for the HFC;

children.title: (i) Outsourcing agreements should include clauses to allow the National Housing
children.text:
(i) Outsourcing agreements should include clauses to allow the National Housing
Bank or persons authorised by it, to access the HFC's documents, records of
transactions, and other necessary information given to, stored or processed by
the service provider, within a reasonable time.

children.title: (j) Outsourcing agreement should also include a clause to recognise the right of the
children.text:
(j) Outsourcing agreement should also include a clause to recognise the right of the
National Housing Bank, to cause an inspection to be made of a service provider
of an HFC and its books and account by one or more of its officers or employees
or other persons.

children.title: (k) The outsourcing agreement should also provide that confidentiality of customer's
children.text:
(k) The outsourcing agreement should also provide that confidentiality of customer's
information should be maintained even after the contract expires or gets
terminated.

children.title: (l) The outsourcing agreement should provide for the preservation of documents and
children.text:
(l) The outsourcing agreement should provide for the preservation of documents and
data by the service provider in accordance with the legal/ regulatory obligation of
the HFC in this regard.
(16) Termination of Agreement

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (i) A termination clause and minimum period to execute a termination provision, should
children.text:
(i) A termination clause and minimum period to execute a termination provision, should
be included.

children.title: (ii) The agreement shall automatically be terminated unless renewed by a fresh
children.text:
(ii) The agreement shall automatically be terminated unless renewed by a fresh
contract by the HFC immediately after the expiry of the period of agreement.

children.title: (iii) No DSA/ DMA shall be allowed to do any fresh business on behalf of the HFC after
children.text:
(iii) No DSA/ DMA shall be allowed to do any fresh business on behalf of the HFC after
termination of agreement until and unless renewed by a fresh agreement.
(17) Where DSA/ DMA is seeking of engagement/ empanelment with the HFC, it or/and
its employees/ representative will have to go through two-day preliminary training and
a day training every year which shall be organized by the HFC. HFC shall also
maintain record of training provided by them.
(18) The HFC availing the services of DSA/ DMA shall maintain up-to-date database of
DSAs/ DMAs engaged/ empaneled with them. HFC shall keep the inspection report
of the inspection conducted in terms of the provisions of the agreement entered into
with the DSA/ DMA, and action taken report (ATR) thereon.
(19) General

children.title: (i) The HFC shall, at least on an annual basis review the financial and operational
children.text:
(i) The HFC shall, at least on an annual basis review the financial and operational
conditions of the service provider to assess their ability to continue to meet their
outsourcing obligations. Such due diligence reviews, which can be based on all
available information about service provider, shall highlight any deterioration or
breach in performance standard confidentially and security, and in business
continuity preparedness.

children.title: (ii) The HFC shall have in place a management structure to monitor and control the
children.text:
(ii) The HFC shall have in place a management structure to monitor and control the
outsourcing activities. It shall ensure that outsourcing agreements with the service
providers contain provisions to address their monitoring and control of outsource
activities.

children.title: (iii) Regular audits by either the internal auditors or external auditors of the HFC shall
children.text:
(iii) Regular audits by either the internal auditors or external auditors of the HFC shall
assess the adequacy of the risk management practices adopted in overseeing and
managing the outsourcing arrangement, the HFC's compliance with its risk
management framework and the requirements of these guidelines.

children.title: (iv) In the event of termination of the agreement for any reason, this shall be publicized
children.text:
(iv) In the event of termination of the agreement for any reason, this shall be publicized
so as to ensure that the customers do not continue to deal with that service provider.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (v) The HFC shall constitute a Grievance Redressal Machinery within the company and
children.text:
(v) The HFC shall constitute a Grievance Redressal Machinery within the company and
give wide publicity about it through electronic and print media.

children.title: (vi) The name and contact number of the designated Grievance Redressal Officer of the
children.text:
(vi) The name and contact number of the designated Grievance Redressal Officer of the
HFC should be made known and widely publicized.

children.title: (vii) The designated officer should ensure that genuine grievances of customers are
children.text:
(vii) The designated officer should ensure that genuine grievances of customers are
redressed promptly without involving delay.

children.title: (viii) It shall be clearly indicated that HFC's Grievance Redressal Machinery will also
children.text:
(viii) It shall be clearly indicated that HFC's Grievance Redressal Machinery will also
deal with the issue relating to services provided by the outsourced agency.

children.title: (ix) Generally, a time limit of one month may be given to the customers for preferring the
children.text:
(ix) Generally, a time limit of one month may be given to the customers for preferring the
complaints/grievances. The grievance redressal procedure of the HFC and the time
frame fixed for responding to the complaints shall be placed on the HFC's website.

children.title: (x) No payment to DSAs/ DMAs shall be made in cash. The fee, incentive etc. shall be
children.text:
(x) No payment to DSAs/ DMAs shall be made in cash. The fee, incentive etc. shall be
made only by way of direct credit to their bank account.

children.title: (xi) HFC, at its discretion, may prescribe the operational area for DSA/ DMA within which
children.text:
(xi) HFC, at its discretion, may prescribe the operational area for DSA/ DMA within which
they can work.

children.title: (xii) The lead shall be shared by the DSA/ DMA in the illustrative format given at Annex
children.text:
(xii) The lead shall be shared by the DSA/ DMA in the illustrative format given at Annex
III.

children.title: (xiii) By virtue of contract/ agreement, the DSA/ DMA or/and its employees/
children.text:
(xiii) By virtue of contract/ agreement, the DSA/ DMA or/and its employees/
representatives may have access to personal and business information of HFC
and/or HFC's customer. DSA/ DMA shall ensure the preservation and protection of
the security and confidentiality of the customer information or data which are in the
custody or possession.

children.title: (xiv) DSA/ DMA shall acknowledge that he/ she/ it has read the said Model Code of
children.text:
(xiv) DSA/ DMA shall acknowledge that he/ she/ it has read the said Model Code of
Conduct and has fully understood all the terms and conditions mentioned there in
and declare that the DSA/ DMA shall agree to abide by the said Code of conduct in
letter and spirit.

children.title: (xv) The DSA/ DMA shall report the fraud committed by erring employees/
children.text:
(xv) The DSA/ DMA shall report the fraud committed by erring employees/
representatives periodically to HFC and consolidated data/ information on the same
shall be submitted by HFC to NHB. The information shall include name of the
person, address, name of the DSA associated with and nature of fraud. Such

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
employees/ representatives shall be barred permanently for doing the business of
DSA/ DMA in future with HFC.

children.title: 157. In the event of receipt of any complaint from the customer that the HFC's
children.text:
157. In the event of receipt of any complaint from the customer that the HFC's
representative/ courier or DSA has engaged in any improper conduct or acted in violation
of this Code, appropriate steps shall be initiated to investigate and to handle the complaint
and to make good the loss.
A.12 Guarantors

children.title: 158. When a person is considering being a guarantor to a loan, they should be informed
159. The HFC shall keep them informed of any material adverse change/s in the
children.text:
159. The HFC shall keep them informed of any material adverse change/s in the
financial position of the borrower to whom he/ she stands as a guarantor.
A.13 Privacy and Confidentiality

children.title: 160. All personal information of customers, both present and past, shall be treated as
children.text:
160. All personal information of customers, both present and past, shall be treated as
private and confidential and shall be guided by the following principles and policies.
(1) The HFC shall not reveal information or data relating to customer accounts,
whether provided by the customers or otherwise, to anyone, including other

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
companies/ entities in their group, other than in the following exceptional
cases:

children.title: (i) If the information is to be given by law.
children.text: (i) If the information is to be given by law.

children.title: (ii) If there is a duty towards the public to reveal the information.
children.text: (ii) If there is a duty towards the public to reveal the information.

children.title: (iii) If the HFC's interests require them to give the information (for example, to
children.text:
(iii) If the HFC's interests require them to give the information (for example, to
prevent fraud). However, it shall not be used as a reason for giving
information about customer or customer accounts (including customer
name and address) to anyone else, including other companies in the
group, for marketing purposes.

children.title: (iv) If the customer asks the HFC to reveal the information, or with the
children.text:
(iv) If the customer asks the HFC to reveal the information, or with the
customer's permission.

children.title: (v) If the HFC is asked to give a reference about customers, it shall obtain
children.text:
(v) If the HFC is asked to give a reference about customers, it shall obtain
their written permission before giving it.
(2) The customer shall be informed the extent of their rights under the existing
legal framework for accessing the personal records that the HFC holds about
them.
(3) The HFC shall not use customer's personal information for marketing purposes
by anyone including HFC, unless the customer specifically authorises it to do
so.
A.14 General

children.title: 161. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraph 18 to 20 and 100 of Reserve
children.text:
161. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraph 18 to 20 and 100 of Reserve
Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Responsible Business Conduct)
Directions, 2025

children.title: 162. Whenever loans are given, the HFC shall explain to the customer the repayment
children.text:
162. Whenever loans are given, the HFC shall explain to the customer the repayment
process by way of amount, tenure and periodicity of repayment. However, if the customer
does not adhere to repayment schedule, a defined process in accordance with the laws
of the land shall be followed for recovery of dues. The process will involve reminding the
customer by sending him/ her notice or by making personal visits and/or repossession of
security if any.

children.title: 163. The HFC shall not charge pre-payment levy or penalty on pre-closure of housing
children.text:
163. The HFC shall not charge pre-payment levy or penalty on pre-closure of housing
loans under the following situations:

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(1) Where the housing loan is on floating interest rate basis and pre-closed from
any source.
(2) Where the housing loan is on fixed interest rate basis and the loan is preclosed
by the borrower out of their own sources.

children.title: Note: The expression "own sources" for the purpose means any source
children.text:
Note: The expression "own sources" for the purpose means any source
other than by borrowing from a bank/ HFC/ NBFC and/or a financial
institution.
(3) All dual/ special rate (combination of fixed and floating) housing loans will attract
the pre-closure norms applicable to fixed/ floating rate depending on whether at
the time of pre-closure, the loan is on fixed or floating rate.
(4) In case of a dual/ special rate housing loans, the pre-closure norm for floating
rate will apply once the loan has been converted into floating rate loan, after the
expiry of the fixed interest rate period. This applied to all such dual/ special rate
housing loans being foreclosed hereafter.
(5) It is also clarified that a fixed rate loan is one where the rate is fixed for entire
duration of the loan.

children.title: 164. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraph 33 to 34 of Reserve Bank of
children.text:
164. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraph 33 to 34 of Reserve Bank of
India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions,
2025.

children.title: 165. Further, to facilitate quick and good understanding of the other major terms and
children.text:
165. Further, to facilitate quick and good understanding of the other major terms and
conditions of housing loan agreed upon between HFC and the individual borrower, the
HFC shall additionally obtain a document containing the other most important terms and
conditions (MITC) of such loan (i.e., other than the details included in KFS) in all cases in
the suggestive format as per the format given in Annex IV. The document will be in
addition to the existing loan and security documents being obtained by the HFC. HFC is
advised to prepare the said document in duplicate and in the language understandable
by the borrower. Duplicate copy duly executed between the HFC and the borrower should
be handed over to the borrower under acknowledgement.

children.title: 166. Display of various key aspect such as service charges, interest rates, Penal
children.text:
166. Display of various key aspect such as service charges, interest rates, Penal
charges (if any), services offered, product information, time norms for various transactions
and grievance redressal mechanism, etc., is required to promote transparency in the

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
operations of HFC. The HFC shall follow the instructions on "Notice Board", "Booklets/
Brochures", "Website", "Other Modes of Display" and on "Other Issues" as per Annex IV.

children.title: 167. The HFC shall display about its products and services in any one or more of the
children.text:
167. The HFC shall display about its products and services in any one or more of the
following languages: Hindi, English or the appropriate local language.

children.title: 168. The HFC shall not discriminate on grounds of sex, caste and religion in the matter
children.text:
168. The HFC shall not discriminate on grounds of sex, caste and religion in the matter
of lending. Further, the HFC shall also not discriminate visually impaired or physically
challenged applicants on the ground of disability in extending products, services, facilities,
etc. However, this does not preclude the HFC from instituting or participating in schemes
framed for different sections of the society.

children.title: 169. To publicise the Code, the HFC shall:
children.text:
169. To publicise the Code, the HFC shall:
(1) provide existing and new customers with a copy of the Code;
(2) make this Code available on request either over the counter or by electronic
communication or mail;
(3) make available this Code at every branch and on their website; and
(4) ensure that their staff are trained to provide relevant information about the Code
and to put the Code into practice.
A.15 Guidelines for engaging Recovery Agents

children.title: 170. The HFC shall adopt the following guidelines for engaging Recovery Agents:
children.text:
170. The HFC shall adopt the following guidelines for engaging Recovery Agents:
(1) The HFC, as principal, shall be responsible for the action of their agents. Hence, it
shall ensure that its agents, engaged for recovery of its dues shall strictly adhere to these
guidelines and instruction including the fair practice code for HFC, while engaging in the
process of recovery of dues.

children.title: Note:
children.text: Note:

children.title: (i) 'Agents' for the purpose of these guidelines would include agencies engaged by
children.text:
(i) 'Agents' for the purpose of these guidelines would include agencies engaged by
the HFC and the agents/ employees of the concerned agencies.

children.title: (ii) It is expected that the HFC would, in the normal course, ensure that their own
children.text:
(ii) It is expected that the HFC would, in the normal course, ensure that their own
employees also adhere to these guidelines during the loan recovery process.
(2) Engagement of Recovery Agents

children.title: (i) The HFC shall have a due diligence process in place for engagement of recovery
children.text:
(i) The HFC shall have a due diligence process in place for engagement of recovery
agents, which shall be structured to cover, among others, individuals involved in the
recovery process.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (ii) The HFC shall also ensure that the agents engaged by them in the recovery
children.text:
(ii) The HFC shall also ensure that the agents engaged by them in the recovery
process, carry out verification of the antecedents of their employees, which may
include pre-employment police verification, as a matter of abundant caution and
HFC may decide the periodicity at which re-verification of antecedents should be
resorted to.
(3) Training of Recovery Agents

children.title: (i) The HFC shall ensure that, among others, the recovery agents are properly trained
children.text:
(i) The HFC shall ensure that, among others, the recovery agents are properly trained
to handle with care and sensitivity, their responsibilities, in particular aspects like
hours of calling, privacy of customer information etc.

children.title: (ii) The HFC shall ensure that over a period of one year, all their recovery agents
children.text:
(ii) The HFC shall ensure that over a period of one year, all their recovery agents
undergo the training as prescribed by Indian Institute of Banking and Finance (IIBF)
and obtain the certificate from the institute.

children.title: (iii) Further, the service providers engaged by the HFC, shall also employ only such
children.text:
(iii) Further, the service providers engaged by the HFC, shall also employ only such
personnel who have undergone the above training and obtained the certificate from
the IIBF.
(4) Intimating borrowers about recovery agents

children.title: (i) HFC shall prominently display the list of recovery agency firms/ companies/
children.text:
(i) HFC shall prominently display the list of recovery agency firms/ companies/
individual as the case may be, empanelled with it including name and period of
empanelment on its website, branches/ offices, mobile applications or any other
platform which is being used for engagement with customer.

children.title: (ii) To ensure due notice and appropriate authorization, the HFC shall inform the
children.text:
(ii) To ensure due notice and appropriate authorization, the HFC shall inform the
borrower, the details of recovery agency firms/ companies while forwarding default
cases to the recovery agency.

children.title: (iii) Further, in some of the cases, the borrower might not have received the details about
children.text:
(iii) Further, in some of the cases, the borrower might not have received the details about
the recovery agency due to refusal/ non-availability/ avoidance.

children.title: (iv) To ensure identification, the agent shall also carry a copy of the notice and the
children.text:
(iv) To ensure identification, the agent shall also carry a copy of the notice and the
authorization letter from the HFC along with the identity card issued to him by the
HFC or the agency firm/ company.

children.title: (v) Where the recovery agency is changed by the HFC during the recovery process, in
children.text:
(v) Where the recovery agency is changed by the HFC during the recovery process, in
addition to the HFC notifying the borrower of the change, the new agent shall carry
the notice and the authorization letter with his identity card.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (vi) The notice and the authorization letter shall, among other details, also include the
children.text:
(vi) The notice and the authorization letter shall, among other details, also include the
telephone numbers of the relevant recovery agency. The HFC shall ensure that
there is a tape recording of the content/ text of the calls made by the recovery agents
to the customers and vice-versa, with the knowledge of the customer. The HFC shall
take reasonable precautions such as intimating the customer that the conversation
is being recorded, etc.
(5) Incentives to Recovery Agents

children.title: (i) Stiff targets or high incentives may induce agents to use intimidatory and
children.text:
(i) Stiff targets or high incentives may induce agents to use intimidatory and
questionable methods for recovery of dues.

children.title: (ii) The HFC shall ensure that the contracts with the recovery agent do not induce
children.text:
(ii) The HFC shall ensure that the contracts with the recovery agent do not induce
adoption of uncivilised, unlawful and questionable behaviour or recovery process.
(6) Methods followed by Recovery Agents

children.title: (i) All the members of the staff or any person authorised to represent the HFC in
children.text:
(i) All the members of the staff or any person authorised to represent the HFC in
collection or/and security repossession shall follow the guidelines set out, such as:

children.title: (a) Customer would be contacted ordinarily at the place of their choice and in the
children.text:
(a) Customer would be contacted ordinarily at the place of their choice and in the
absence of any specified place at the place of their residence and if unavailable
at their residence, at the place of business/ occupation.

children.title: (b) Identity and authority to represent the HFC shall be made known to the customer
children.text:
(b) Identity and authority to represent the HFC shall be made known to the customer
at the first instance.

children.title: (c) Customer's privacy shall be respected.
children.text: (c) Customer's privacy shall be respected.

children.title: (d) Interaction with the customer shall be in a civil manner.
children.text: (d) Interaction with the customer shall be in a civil manner.

children.title: (e) HFCs' representatives shall contact customers between 8.00 a.m. to 7.00 p.m.
children.text:
(e) HFCs' representatives shall contact customers between 8.00 a.m. to 7.00 p.m.
for recovery of overdue loans.

children.title: (f) Customer's request to avoid call at a particular time or at a particular place shall
children.text:
(f) Customer's request to avoid call at a particular time or at a particular place shall
be honoured as far as possible.

children.title: (g) The time and number of calls and contents of conversation shall be
children.text:
(g) The time and number of calls and contents of conversation shall be
documented.

children.title: (h) All assistance shall be given to resolve disputes or differences regarding dues
children.text:
(h) All assistance shall be given to resolve disputes or differences regarding dues
in a mutually acceptable and in an orderly manner.

children.title: (i) During visits to customer's place for dues collection, decency and decorum shall
children.text:
(i) During visits to customer's place for dues collection, decency and decorum shall
be maintained.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (j) Only employees of the Recovery Agency shall visit the borrower's premises for
children.text:
(j) Only employees of the Recovery Agency shall visit the borrower's premises for
the recovery/ collection activity and no other person shall accompany such
Recovery Agent.

children.title: (k) Inappropriate occasions such as bereavement in the family or such other
children.text:
(k) Inappropriate occasions such as bereavement in the family or such other
calamitous occasion, or marriage functions, festivals etc. shall be avoided for
making calls/ visits to collect dues.

children.title: (l) The written communication sent by the collection agent to the borrower shall
children.text:
(l) The written communication sent by the collection agent to the borrower shall
have the approval of the HFC.

children.title: (m) The HFC shall interact only with the customer/ borrower or the guarantor (only
children.text:
(m) The HFC shall interact only with the customer/ borrower or the guarantor (only
if so required) and shall not approach any other relatives/ contacts of the
borrower.
(7) Taking possession of property mortgaged to HFCs

children.title: (i) It has been observed by the Hon'ble Supreme Court that we are governed by rule
children.text:
(i) It has been observed by the Hon'ble Supreme Court that we are governed by rule
of law in the country and the recovery of loans or seizure of asset could be done
only through legal means.

children.title: (ii) It is emphasised in this context that the HFC may rely on legal remedies available
children.text:
(ii) It is emphasised in this context that the HFC may rely on legal remedies available
under the relevant statutes while enforcing security interest without intervention of
the courts. In this context, it may be mentioned that the Securitization and
Reconstruction of Financial Assets and Enforcement of Security Interest Act, 2002
(SARFAESI Act) and the Security Interest (Enforcement) Rules, 2002, framed
thereunder have laid down well-defined procedures not only for enforcing security
interest but also for auctioning the movable and immovable property after enforcing
the security interest.

children.title: (iii) Where the HFC has incorporated a pre-possession clause in the contract with the
children.text:
(iii) Where the HFC has incorporated a pre-possession clause in the contract with the
borrower and rely on such pre-possession clause for enforcing their rights, they shall
ensure that the pre-possession clause is legally valid, complies with the provisions
of the Indian Contract Act in letter and spirit, and ensure that such prepossession
clause is clearly brought to the notice of the borrower at the time of execution of the
contract. The terms and condition of the contract shall be strictly in terms of the
disclosed recovery policy and should contain provisions regarding:

children.title: (a) Notice period before taking possession;
children.text: (a) Notice period before taking possession;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (b) circumstances under which the notice period can be waived;
children.text: (b) circumstances under which the notice period can be waived;

children.title: (c) the procedure for taking possession of the security;
children.text: (c) the procedure for taking possession of the security;

children.title: (d) a provision regarding final chance to be given to the borrower for repayment
children.text:
(d) a provision regarding final chance to be given to the borrower for repayment
of loan before the sale/ auction of the property;

children.title: (e) the procedure for giving repossession to the borrower; and
children.text: (e) the procedure for giving repossession to the borrower; and

children.title: (f) the procedure for sale/ auction of the property.
children.text:
(f) the procedure for sale/ auction of the property.
(8) Use of forum of Lok Adalats

children.title: (i) The Honourable Supreme Court has also observed, inter alia, that loans, personal
children.text:
(i) The Honourable Supreme Court has also observed, inter alia, that loans, personal
loans, credit card loans and housing loans with less than ₹10 lakh can be referred
to Lok Adalats.

children.title: (ii) The HFC is encouraged to use the forum of Lok Adalats for recovery of housing
children.text:
(ii) The HFC is encouraged to use the forum of Lok Adalats for recovery of housing
loans with less than ₹10 lakh as suggested by Honourable Supreme Court.
(9) Utilisation of Credit Counsellors

children.title: (i) The HFC shall have in place an appropriate mechanism to utilize the service of credit
children.text:
(i) The HFC shall have in place an appropriate mechanism to utilize the service of credit
counsellors for providing suitable counselling to the borrowers where they become
aware that the case of a particular borrower deserves sympathetic consideration.
(10) Complaints against the HFC / its Recovery Agents

children.title: (i) Complaints received by NHB regarding violation of the above guidelines and
children.text:
(i) Complaints received by NHB regarding violation of the above guidelines and
adoption of above practices followed by recovery agents of HFC would be viewed
seriously.

children.title: (ii) Supervisory actions could be attracted when the High Courts or the Supreme Court
children.text:
(ii) Supervisory actions could be attracted when the High Courts or the Supreme Court
pass strictures or impose penalties against any HFC or its Directors/ Officers/ agents
with regard to policy, practice and procedures related to the recovery process.

children.title: (iii) Where a grievance/ complaint has been lodged, the HFC shall not forward cases to
children.text:
(iii) Where a grievance/ complaint has been lodged, the HFC shall not forward cases to
recovery agencies till they have finally disposed of any grievance/ complaint lodged
by the concerned borrower. However, where the HFC is convinced, with appropriate
proof, that the borrower is continuously making frivolous/ vexatious complaints, it
may continue with the recovery proceedings through the Recovery Agents even if a
grievance/ complaint is pending with them.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (iv) In case where the subject matter of the borrower's dues might be sub judice, the
children.text:
(iv) In case where the subject matter of the borrower's dues might be sub judice, the
HFC shall exercise utmost caution, as appropriate, in referring the matter to the
recovery agencies, depending on the circumstances.

children.title: (v) Each HFC shall have a mechanism whereby the borrower grievances with regards
children.text:
(v) Each HFC shall have a mechanism whereby the borrower grievances with regards
to the recovery process can be addressed. The details of the mechanism shall also
be furnished to the borrower while advising the details of the recovery agency as at
sub-paragraph (iv) above.
(11) Periodical review, monitoring and control

children.title: (i) An HFC engaging recovery agents shall undertake a periodical review of the
children.text:
(i) An HFC engaging recovery agents shall undertake a periodical review of the
mechanism to learn from experience, to effect improvement and to bring to the
notice of the Reserve Bank suggestion for improvement in the guidelines.
(12) General

children.title: (i) An HFC shall, at least on an annual basis, review the financial and operation
children.text:
(i) An HFC shall, at least on an annual basis, review the financial and operation
condition of the service providers to assess their ability to continue to meet their
outsourcing conditions. Such due diligence reviews, which can be based on all
available information about the service provider should highlight any deterioration
or breach in performance standards, confidentiality and security, and in business
continuity preparedness.

children.title: (ii) The outsourcing agreement shall provide for the prohibition of further outsourcing
children.text:
(ii) The outsourcing agreement shall provide for the prohibition of further outsourcing
by the service provider for all or part of an outsourced activity;

children.title: (iii) An HFC shall have in place a management structure to monitor and control its
children.text:
(iii) An HFC shall have in place a management structure to monitor and control its
outsourcing activities. It shall ensure that outsourcing agreement with the service
providers contain provisions to address their monitoring and control of outsourced
activities.

children.title: (iv) Regular audits by either the internal auditors or external auditors of the HFC shall,
children.text:
(iv) Regular audits by either the internal auditors or external auditors of the HFC shall,
inter alia, assess the adequacy of the risk management practices adopted in
overseeing and managing the outsourcing arrangement, the HFC's compliance with
its risk management framework and the requirements of these guidelines.

children.title: (v) In the event of termination of the agreement for any reason, this shall be publicized
children.text:
(v) In the event of termination of the agreement for any reason, this shall be publicized
so as to ensure that the customers do not continue to deal with that service
providers.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (vi) The HFC shall constitute a Grievance Redressal Machinery within the company and
children.text:
(vi) The HFC shall constitute a Grievance Redressal Machinery within the company and
give wide publicity about it through electronic and print media.

children.title: (vii) The name and contact number of designated grievance redressal officer of the HFC
children.text:
(vii) The name and contact number of designated grievance redressal officer of the HFC
shall be made known and widely publicised.

children.title: (viii) The designated officer shall ensure that genuine grievances of customers are
children.text:
(viii) The designated officer shall ensure that genuine grievances of customers are
redressed promptly without any delay. It shall be clearly indicated that HFC
Grievance Redressal Machinery will also deal with the issue relating to services
provided by the outsourced agency.

children.title: (ix) Generally, a time limit of one month may be given to the customer for preferring their
children.text:
(ix) Generally, a time limit of one month may be given to the customer for preferring their
complaints/ grievances. The grievance redressal procedure of the HFC and the time
frame fixed for responding to the complaints shall be placed on the HFC's website.
A.16 Loan facilities to the physically/ visually challenged

children.title: 171. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraph 28 of Reserve Bank of India
children.text:
171. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraph 28 of Reserve Bank of India
(Non-Banking Financial Companies - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025.

children.title: 172. The HFC shall adopt the following illustrative guidelines for loan facilities to visually
children.text:
172. The HFC shall adopt the following illustrative guidelines for loan facilities to visually
challenged applicant:
(1) All products, services, facilities, etc. offered by the HFC shall be made available
to visually impaired persons and shall be offered at all branches/ offices of the
HFC.
(2) All products, services, facilities, etc. must be made available to visually impaired
customers as are offered to other customers and their impairment of vision
should not be a criterion for sanctioning/ denying a loan.
(3) The HFC must provide the same facilities to a visually impaired customer as it
would to any other customer.
(4) The HFC must follow the same procedure for extending products, services,
facilities, etc. offered by them to a visually impaired customer as it does for its
other customers.
(5) No additional burden of interest payment, collateral and other terms shall be
imposed on the visually impaired customer.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(6) If the credit policy of an HFC does not insist for a co-borrower or a guarantor for
other customers for any type of loan facilities extended by it, the same shall not
be insisted upon for a visually impaired customer.
(7) The HFC shall not equate visually impaired customers with illiterate customers.
(8) The HFC shall not deny any services to visually impaired customers including
visually impaired customers who use their thumb impression. If necessary, HFC,
at its discretion, may take a Declaration of Thumb Impression as an additional
document from visually impaired customer.
(9) Additional facilities like reading and filling up of forms, slips, etc. shall be
provided to a visually impaired customer. The Officer/ Manager of the branch/
office shall read out the rules of business and other terms and conditions in the
presence of a witness, if required by the customer.
(10) HFC must allow the visually impaired customer to take a loan or avail any other
facilities offered by them jointly with anybody that he/ she chooses including
person(s) who is/are visually impaired.
(11) Visually impaired customers may be allowed to appoint a person/ persons as
their Power of Attorney or Mandate Holder to operate their account, if the visually
impaired customer so desires.
(12) The Officer/ Manager of the branch/ office must inform a visually impaired
customer/ prospective customer of his rights and liabilities before offering the
product.
(13) The documentation requirements of a visually impaired customer must be the
same as any other customer. The account has to be clearly marked as "the
account holder is visually impaired".
(14) The HFC shall provide a copy of all documents to visually impaired customer in
digital form also, if required.
(15) The HFC shall provide a copy of the KFS and other Most Important Terms and
Conditions (MITC) as applicable, to visually impaired customer in braille form or
text readable PDF, if so desired by them.
(16) The HFC shall preferably provide Electronic Clearing Service (ECS) facility to
the visually impaired customer.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(17) It should be noted that these guidelines are only illustrative and by no measures
exhaustive.
A.17 Providing facilities to persons with disabilities: Compliance with Hon'ble
Supreme Court Order dated April 30, 2025 in the matter of Pragya Prasun and Ors.
vs Union of India (W.P.(C) 289 of 2024) and Amar Jain vs Union of India & Ors.
(W.P.(C) 49 of 2025)

Key Facts Statement (KFS) for Loans & Advances

Chapter-XI - Miscellaneous Instructions
A.
Finance for Housing Projects

children.title: 178. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraph 108 of Reserve Bank of India
children.text:
178. HFCs shall comply with the provisions of paragraph 108 of Reserve Bank of India
(Non-Banking Financial Companies - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025.
A.1 Housing Loans

children.title: 179. Housing Loan for Building Construction
children.text:
179. Housing Loan for Building Construction
(1) In cases where the applicant owns a plot / land and approaches the HFC for a
credit facility to construct a house, a copy of the sanctioned plan by competent
authority in the name of the person applying for such credit facility must be
obtained by the HFC before sanctioning the home loan.
(2) An affidavit-cum-undertaking must be obtained from the person applying for such
credit facility that he shall not violate the sanctioned plan, the construction shall
be strictly as per the sanctioned plan and it shall be the sole responsibility of the
executant to obtain completion certificate within 3 months of completion of
construction, failing which the HFC shall have the power and the authority to recall
the entire loan with interest, costs and other usual bank charges.
(3) An architect appointed by the HFC must certify at various stages of construction
of building that the construction is strictly as per sanctioned plan. He/ She shall
also certify, at a particular point of time that the completion certificate of the
building issued by the competent authority has been obtained.

children.title: 180. H
children.text:
180. H
(1) In cases where the applicant approaches the HFC for a credit facility to purchase
the built-up house/ flat, it should be mandatory for him to declare by way of an
effective affidavit-cum-undertaking that built up house has been constructed as
per the sanctioned plan and/or building by-laws and as far as possible has a
completion certificate also.
(2) An Architect appointed by the HFC must also certify before disbursement of the
loan that built up house is strictly as per the sanctioned plan and/or building by-
laws.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 181. No loan shall be given in respect of those properties which fall in the category of
children.text:
181. No loan shall be given in respect of those properties which fall in the category of
unauthorised colonies unless and until they have been regularized and development and
other charges paid.

children.title: 182. No loan shall also be given in respect of properties meant for residential use but
children.text:
182. No loan shall also be given in respect of properties meant for residential use but
which the applicant intends to use for commercial purposes and declares so while
applying for loan.
A.2 Disbursement of housing loan to individuals linked to the stages of
construction

children.title: 183. Disbursal of housing loans sanctioned to individuals shall be strictly linked to the
children.text:
183. Disbursal of housing loans sanctioned to individuals shall be strictly linked to the
stages of construction of the housing projects/ houses and upfront disbursal shall not be
made in case of incomplete/ under-construction/ green field housing project/ houses.

children.title: 184. The HFC while introducing any kind of product shall take into account the customer
children.text:
184. The HFC while introducing any kind of product shall take into account the customer
suitability and appropriateness issues and also ensure that the borrowers/ customers are
made fully aware of the risk and liabilities under such products.

children.title: 185. In cases of projects sponsored by Government/ Statutory Authorities, The HFC
children.text:
185. In cases of projects sponsored by Government/ Statutory Authorities, The HFC
may disburse the loans as per the payment stages prescribed by such authorities, even
where payments sought from house buyers are not linked to the stages of construction,
provided such authorities have no past history of non-completion of projects.

children.title: 186. The HFC shall desist from offering loan products involving servicing of the loan
children.text:
186. The HFC shall desist from offering loan products involving servicing of the loan
dues by builders/ developers etc. on behalf of the borrowers.

children.title: 187. The HFC shall have in place a well-defined mechanism for effective monitoring of
children.text:
187. The HFC shall have in place a well-defined mechanism for effective monitoring of
the progress of construction of housing projects and obtaining consent of the borrower(s)
prior to release of payments to the builder/developer.

children.title: 188. The HFC while extending finance shall take into account the stipulations laid down
children.text:
188. The HFC while extending finance shall take into account the stipulations laid down
under RERA, as applicable.
A.3 Need for ensuring due diligence in the matter of deployment of funds by HFCs

children.title: 189. The HFC shall take proper and adequate security for the loans. In case a loan is
children.text:
189. The HFC shall take proper and adequate security for the loans. In case a loan is
to be sanctioned on unsecured basis, the same shall be in accordance with the Board
approved policy of the HFC.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 190. The HFC shall review and strengthen their credit appraisal systems. Wherever
children.text:
190. The HFC shall review and strengthen their credit appraisal systems. Wherever
documents of title are submitted as security for loans, there should be a system of
verification of their genuineness, especially in large value loans. Wherever a chartered
accountant certificate, property valuation certificate, legal certificate, guarantee/ line of
credit or any other third-party certification is submitted by the borrower, the HFC shall
independently verify the authenticity of such certification by directly communicating with
the concerned authority issuing the certificate. Indirect confirmation may also be resorted
to, i.e. indicating to the issuer that in case there is no response by certain deadline, it
would be assumed that the certificate is genuine.

children.title: 191. The HFC shall ensure that the documents are not given directly to the customers
children.text:
191. The HFC shall ensure that the documents are not given directly to the customers
for verification, etc. to obviate any frauds.

children.title: 192. The HFC shall ensure that the borrowers have obtained all required permissions/
children.text:
192. The HFC shall ensure that the borrowers have obtained all required permissions/
clearances from Government/ Local Government/ Statutory Authorities for the project. In
case of construction loans, there should be system in place for physical verification/
