children.title: Annex I
children.text:
Annex I
Terms and Conditions applicable to Hybrid Debt Capital Instruments to qualify for
inclusion as Tier 2 Capital

children.title: 1. Currency of Issue
children.text: 1. Currency of Issue

children.title: 1.1. HFCs shall issue Tier 2 instruments in Indian Rupees.
children.text: 1.1. HFCs shall issue Tier 2 instruments in Indian Rupees.

children.title: 1.2. HFCs shall obtain prior approval of the Reserve Bank, on a case-by-case basis,
children.text:
1.2. HFCs shall obtain prior approval of the Reserve Bank, on a case-by-case basis,
for issue of a Tier 2 Instruments in foreign currency.

children.title: 2. Amount
children.text:
2. Amount
The amount to be raised by issue of such instruments may be decided by the Board
of Directors of HFCs.

children.title: 3. Limits
children.text:
3. Limits
The aggregate amount of such instruments along with other components of Tier 2
capital shall not exceed 100% of Tier 1 capital. This eligible amount will be computed
with reference to the amount of Tier 1 capital as on March 31st of the previous
financial year, after deduction of goodwill and other intangible assets but before the
deduction of investments.

children.title: 4. Maturity period
children.text:
4. Maturity period
The instruments should have a minimum maturity of 15 years.

children.title: 5. Rate of Interest
children.text:
5. Rate of Interest
The interest payable to the investors may be either at a fixed rate or at a floating rate
referenced to a market determined rupee interest benchmark rate.

children.title: 6. Options
children.text: 6. Options

children.title: 6.1. The debt instruments shall not be issued with 'put option'.
children.text: 6.1. The debt instruments shall not be issued with 'put option'.

children.title: 6.2. HFCs may issue the instruments with 'call option' subject to strict compliance with
children.text:
6.2. HFCs may issue the instruments with 'call option' subject to strict compliance with
each of the following conditions:
a. Call option may be exercised only if the instrument has run for at least 10 years;
b. Call option shall be exercised only with the prior approval of NHB. While
considering the proposals received from HFCs for exercising the call option,
HFC's capital to risk assets ratio (CRAR) position both at the time of exercise of
the call option and after exercise of call option shall be considered.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 7. Step Up
children.text:
7. Step Up
The issuing housing finance company may have a step-up option which may be
exercised only once during the whole life of the instrument, in conjunction with the
call option after the lapse of ten years from date of issue. The step up shall not be
more than 100 bps. The limits on step up apply to the all-in cost of the debt to the
issuing HFCs.

children.title: 8. Lock-in clause
children.text: 8. Lock-in clause

children.title: 8.1. The instruments shall be subjected to lock-in clause in terms of which the issuing
children.text:
8.1. The instruments shall be subjected to lock-in clause in terms of which the issuing
HFC shall not be liable to pay either interest or principal, even at maturity, if
a. the HFC's CRAR is below the minimum regulatory requirement prescribed by
the Reserve Bank; OR
b. the impact of such payments results in HFC's CRAR falling below or
remaining below the minimum regulatory requirement prescribed by the
Reserve Bank.

children.title: 8.2. However, HFCs may pay interest with prior approval of NHB, when impact of
children.text:
8.2. However, HFCs may pay interest with prior approval of NHB, when impact of
such payment may result in net loss or increase the net loss, provided CRAR
remains above the regulatory norm.

children.title: 8.3. The interest amount due and remaining unpaid may be allowed to be paid in the
children.text:
8.3. The interest amount due and remaining unpaid may be allowed to be paid in the
later years in cash/ cheque subject to the housing finance company complying
with the above regulatory requirement.

children.title: 8.4. All instances of invocation of the lock-in clause should be notified by the issuing
children.text:
8.4. All instances of invocation of the lock-in clause should be notified by the issuing
HFCs to the NHB.

children.title: 9. Seniority of claim
children.text:
9. Seniority of claim
The claims of the investors in such Tier 2 instruments shall be
a. Superior to the claims of the investors in instruments eligible for inclusion in Tier
1 capital; and
b. Subordinate to the claims of all other creditors.

children.title: 10. Discounting
children.text:
10. Discounting
These instruments shall be subjected to a progressive discount for capital adequacy
purposes as in the case of long-term subordinated debt over the last five years of
their tenor. As they approach maturity these instruments should be subjected to

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
progressive discount as indicated in the table below for being eligible for inclusion in
Tier 2 capital.
Remaining maturity of instruments Rate of Discount (%)
Less than one year 100
One year and more but less than two years 80
Two years and more but less than three years 60
Three years and more but less than four years 40
Four years and more but less than five years 20

children.title: 11. Redemption
children.text: 11. Redemption

children.title: 11.1. These instruments shall not be redeemable at the initiative of the holder.
children.text: 11.1. These instruments shall not be redeemable at the initiative of the holder.

children.title: 11.2. All redemptions shall be made only with prior approval of NHB.
children.text: 11.2. All redemptions shall be made only with prior approval of NHB.

children.title: 12. Reserve Requirements
children.text:
12. Reserve Requirements
Not required

children.title: 13. Investments by FIIs & NRIs
children.text:
13. Investments by FIIs & NRIs
Investments in these instruments by FIIs shall be within the limits as laid down in the
ECB Policy for investments in debt instruments. In addition, NRIs shall also be
eligible to invest in these instruments as per existing policy.

children.title: 14. Issue of Tier 2 instruments in foreign currency
children.text:
14. Issue of Tier 2 instruments in foreign currency
HFCs may augment their capital funds through the issue of such Tier 2 instruments
in foreign currency after seeking prior approval of the Reserve Bank and subject to
compliance with the undermentioned requirements:

children.title: 14.1. The total amount of such Tier 2 instruments in foreign currency shall not exceed
children.text:
14.1. The total amount of such Tier 2 instruments in foreign currency shall not exceed
25% of the unimpaired Tier 1 capital. This eligible amount will be computed with
reference to the amount of Tier 1 capital as on March 31st of the previous financial
year, after deduction of goodwill and other intangible assets but before the
deduction of investments.

children.title: 14.2. Investment by FIIs in such instruments raised in Indian Rupees shall be outside
children.text:
14.2. Investment by FIIs in such instruments raised in Indian Rupees shall be outside
the limit for investments in capital debt instruments. However, investment by FIIs
in these instruments will be subjected to separate ceiling of USD 500 million.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 14.3. HFCs should not enter into swap transactions in respect of these Tier 2
children.text:
14.3. HFCs should not enter into swap transactions in respect of these Tier 2
Instruments.

children.title: 15. Other Conditions
children.text: 15. Other Conditions

children.title: 15.1. These instruments should be fully paid-up, unsecured and free of any restrictive
children.text:
15.1. These instruments should be fully paid-up, unsecured and free of any restrictive
clauses.

children.title: 15.2. HFCs should comply with the terms and conditions, if any, by SEBI / other
children.text:
15.2. HFCs should comply with the terms and conditions, if any, by SEBI / other
regulatory authorities in regard to issue of the instruments.

children.title: 16. Reporting Requirements
children.text:
16. Reporting Requirements
HFCs issuing these instruments shall submit a report to the NHB, giving details of
the debt raised, including the terms of issue together with the copy of the offer
document soon after the issue is completed.

children.title: 17. Grant of advances against such Tier 2 Instruments
children.text:
17. Grant of advances against such Tier 2 Instruments
HFCs should not grant advances against the security of such Tier 2 instruments
issued by them.

children.title: Annex IV
children.text:
Annex IV
Display of Information by HFCs, Key Fact Statement (KFS) & Most Important
Terms and Conditions(MITC)
In order to promote transparency in the operations of HFCs, the following instructions
are issued to HFCs.

children.title: 1. Notice Boards
children.text: 1. Notice Boards

children.title: 1.1. The minimum size of the board may be 2 feet by 2 feet as the board of such a
children.text:
1.1. The minimum size of the board may be 2 feet by 2 feet as the board of such a
size would facilitate comfortable viewing from a distance of 3 to 5 meters. HFCs
are advised to display the information in the notice boards of their branches/
offices as per the format given in the Appendix IV(a) for the comprehensive
notice board.

children.title: 1.2. While displaying the information in the notice board, HFCs may also adhere to
children.text:
1.2. While displaying the information in the notice board, HFCs may also adhere to
the following principles:

children.title: 1.2.1. The notice board may be updated on a periodical basis and the board should
1.2.2. Though the pattern, colour and design of the board is left to the discretion
children.text:
1.2.2. Though the pattern, colour and design of the board is left to the discretion
of the HFCs, yet the display must be simple and readable;

children.title: 1.2.3. The language requirements (i.e., bilingual in Hindi speaking states and
children.text:
1.2.3. The language requirements (i.e., bilingual in Hindi speaking states and
trilingual in other states) may be taken into account;

children.title: 1.2.4. The notice board shall specifically indicate wherever recent changes have
children.text:
1.2.4. The notice board shall specifically indicate wherever recent changes have
been done. For instance, if there is a recent change in the home loan
products offered by the HFC, the information on the home loan products
may be displayed as 'We offer home loans/ products (changed
on…………….. )'; and

children.title: 1.2.5. The notice board may also indicate a list of items on which detailed
children.text:
1.2.5. The notice board may also indicate a list of items on which detailed
information is available in booklet form.

children.title: 1.3. Further, in addition to the above board, the HFCs should also display details such
children.text:
1.3. Further, in addition to the above board, the HFCs should also display details such
as 'Name of the HFC/ branch/ office, Working Days, Working Hours and Weekly
Off-days' outside the branch/ office premises.

children.title: 2. Booklets/ Brochures
children.text: 2. Booklets/ Brochures

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 2.1. The detailed information as indicated in paragraph 1.2.5 above may be made
children.text:
2.1. The detailed information as indicated in paragraph 1.2.5 above may be made
available in various booklets/ brochures as decided by the HFC. These booklets/
brochures may be kept in a separate file/ folder in the form of 'replaceable pages'
so as to facilitate copying and updation. In this connection, HFCs may also
adhere to the following broad guidelines:

children.title: 2.1.1. The file/ folder may be kept at the customer lobby in the branch or at the 'May
children.text:
2.1.1. The file/ folder may be kept at the customer lobby in the branch or at the 'May
I Help You' counter or at a place that is frequented by most of the customers;

children.title: 2.1.2. The language requirements (i.e. bilingual in Hindi speaking states and
children.text:
2.1.2. The language requirements (i.e. bilingual in Hindi speaking states and
trilingual in other states) may be taken into account;

children.title: 2.1.3. While printing the booklets it may be ensured that the font size is minimum
children.text:
2.1.3. While printing the booklets it may be ensured that the font size is minimum
Arial 10 so that the customers are able to easily read the same; and

children.title: 2.1.4. Copies of booklets may be made available to the customers on request.
children.text: 2.1.4. Copies of booklets may be made available to the customers on request.

children.title: 3. Website
children.text: 3. Website

children.title: 3.1. The detailed information as indicated in paragraph 1.2.5 above may also be made
children.text:
3.1. The detailed information as indicated in paragraph 1.2.5 above may also be made
available on the HFC's web-site. HFCs should adhere to the broad guidelines
relating to dating of material, legibility etc., while placing the same on their
websites.

children.title: 3.2. HFCs should display on their website the interest rate range of contracted loans
children.text:
3.2. HFCs should display on their website the interest rate range of contracted loans
for the past quarter for different categories of advances granted to individual
borrowers along with mean interest rates for such loans.

children.title: 3.3. The total fees and charges applicable on various types of loans to individual
children.text:
3.3. The total fees and charges applicable on various types of loans to individual
borrower should be disclosed at the time of processing of loan as well as
displayed on the websites of HFCs for transparency and comparability and to
facilitate informed decision making by customers.

children.title: 3.4. HFCs should publish Annual Percentage Rate (APR) or such similar other
children.text:
3.4. HFCs should publish Annual Percentage Rate (APR) or such similar other
arrangement of representing the total cost of credit on a loan to an individual
borrower on their websites so as to allow customers to compare the costs
associated with borrowing across products and/or lenders.

children.title: 3.5. ln this context, HFCs are also advised to ensure that the customers are able to
children.text:
3.5. ln this context, HFCs are also advised to ensure that the customers are able to
easily access the relevant information from the Home Page of the HFC's
websites. Further, there are certain information relating to service charges, fees

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
and grievance redressal, for which latest updated information are to be posted
compulsorily on the websites of the HFCs.

children.title: 3.6. A format has been devised for display of information relating to interest rates and
children.text:
3.6. A format has been devised for display of information relating to interest rates and
service charges which would enable the customer to obtain the desired
information at a quick glance. The format is given in the Appendix IV(b). HFCs
are advised to display the information as per the format given in the Appendix on
their web-sites. HFCs are however free to modify the format to suit their
requirements, without impairing the basic structure or curtailing the scope of
disclosures.

children.title: 4. Other Modes of Display
children.text:
4. Other Modes of Display
HFCs may also consider displaying all the information that have to be given in
the booklet form in the touch screen by placing them in the Information Kiosks,
Scroll Bars, Tag Boards and/or other options available. The above broad
guidelines may be adhered to, while displaying information using these modes.

children.title: 5. Other Issues
children.text: 5. Other Issues

children.title: 5.1. HFCs are free to decide on their promotional and product information displays.
children.text:
5.1. HFCs are free to decide on their promotional and product information displays.
However, the mandatory displays may not be obstructed in anyway. As customer
interest and financial education are sought to be achieved by the mandatory
display requirements, they should also be given priority over the other display
boards. Information relating to Government sponsored schemes as applicable
location-wise may be displayed according to their applicability.

children.title: 5.2. HFCs should provide KFS as per prescribed format in Reserve Bank of India
children.text:
5.2. HFCs should provide KFS as per prescribed format in Reserve Bank of India
(Non-Banking Financial Companies - Responsible Business Conduct)
Directions, 2025 and also other most important terms and conditions, as per
prescribed format in Appendix IV(b), to all borrowers at every stage of the loan
processing as well as in case of any change in any terms and conditions as per
extant instructions. The same may also be included as a summary box to be
displayed in the credit agreement.

children.title: Annex V
children.text:
Annex V
Valuation of Properties - Empanelment of Valuers
The issue of correct and realistic valuation of properties or fixed assets owned by HFCs
and that accepted by them as security (primary or collateral) for a sizable portion of their
advances' portfolio assumes significance in view of its implications for correct
measurement of capital adequacy position of HFCs. In this context, there is a need for
putting in place a system/ procedure for realistic valuation of properties/ fixed assets
and also for empanelment of valuers for the purpose. HFCs shall be guided by the
following aspects while formulating a policy on valuation of properties and appointment
of valuers:

children.title: 1. Policy for valuation of immovable properties
children.text: 1. Policy for valuation of immovable properties

children.title: 1.1. HFCs shall have a Board approved policy in place for valuation of properties
children.text:
1.1. HFCs shall have a Board approved policy in place for valuation of properties
including collaterals accepted for their exposures.

children.title: 1.2. The valuation shall be done by professionally qualified independent valuers i.e.
children.text:
1.2. The valuation shall be done by professionally qualified independent valuers i.e.
the valuer should not have a direct or indirect interest. However, valuation of
properties by the internal technical valuers of housing finance companies is
permissible subject to the internal technical valuer having qualifications similar
to those prescribed under the Companies (Registered Valuers and Valuation)
Rules, 2017.

children.title: 1.3. The frequency of valuation shall be decided by the Board of an HFC, based on
children.text:
1.3. The frequency of valuation shall be decided by the Board of an HFC, based on
the observed volatility in the prices of the assets in the past except annually in
the case of Non-Performing Asset (NPA). The frequency of valuation in case of
NonPerforming Asset (NPA) shall be annual in case of assets classified as
substandard for more than six months or the classification of assets as doubtful
assets. The frequency decided by the HFC shall be reviewed by its Board
annually. Further, where the value of the properties has been substantially
impaired by any event, these are to be immediately revalued and appropriately
factored in to capital adequacy computation.

children.title: 1.4. Valuation procedure to be followed to ensure that the realisable value of
children.text:
1.4. Valuation procedure to be followed to ensure that the realisable value of
properties is reasonably estimated.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 1.5. HFCs shall obtain minimum two valuation reports, at least one of them being from
children.text:
1.5. HFCs shall obtain minimum two valuation reports, at least one of them being from
an independent valuer, in case the loan amount is ₹50 lakh or above (or such
any other lower value as may be decided by the Board of the company) and
below ₹75 lakh. The lower of the two valuations shall be considered by the HFC
for deciding upon the loan amount.

children.title: 1.6. In case the loan amount is ₹75 lakh or above, HFCs shall necessarily obtain
children.text:
1.6. In case the loan amount is ₹75 lakh or above, HFCs shall necessarily obtain
minimum two independent valuation reports and the lower of the two shall be
considered by the HFC for deciding upon the loan amount.

children.title: 1.7. The requirement of valuation in respect of financing of the initial purchase of a
children.text:
1.7. The requirement of valuation in respect of financing of the initial purchase of a
residential dwelling unit from a State Housing Board/Municipal Corporation/
Developmental Authority or other public agencies by an HFC shall be decided
by the company with the approval of its Board.

children.title: 1.8. In respect of financing of any initial transaction of the purchase of a property, the
children.text:
1.8. In respect of financing of any initial transaction of the purchase of a property, the
value of the property for the purposes of arriving at the Loan to Value ratio (LTV)
should not exceed the documented transaction value as per the agreement to
sale, sale deed etc. Valuation in such cases, if required, may be done as per the
policy approved by the Board of the company.

children.title: 2. Policy of revaluation of HFC's own properties
children.text:
2. Policy of revaluation of HFC's own properties
In addition to the above, the HFCs may keep the following aspects in view while
formulating policy for revaluation of their own properties:

children.title: 2.1. HFCs have been permitted to include revaluation reserves at a discount of 55%
children.text:
2.1. HFCs have been permitted to include revaluation reserves at a discount of 55%
as a part of Tier 2 Capital. In view of this, it is necessary that revaluation reserves
represent true appreciation in the market value of the properties and HFCs have
in place a comprehensive policy for revaluation of fixed assets owned by them.
HFCs shall have a Board approved comprehensive policy in place for valuation
of its own properties and such a policy should inter-alia cover procedure for
identification of assets for revaluation, maintenance of separate set of records
for such assets, the frequency of revaluation, depreciation policy for such assets,
policy for sale of such revalued assets etc. The policy should also cover the
disclosure required to be made in the 'Notes on Account' regarding the details of

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
revaluation such as the original cost of the fixed assets subject to revaluation
and accounting treatment for appreciation/ depreciation etc.

children.title: 2.2. As the revaluation should reflect the change in the fair value of the fixed asset,
children.text:
2.2. As the revaluation should reflect the change in the fair value of the fixed asset,
the frequency of revaluation should be determined based on the observed
volatility in the prices of the assets in the past. Further, any change in the method
of depreciation should reflect the change in the expected pattern of consumption
of the future economic benefits of the assets. The HFCs should adhere to these
principles meticulously while changing the frequency of revaluation/method of
depreciation for a particular class of asset and should make proper disclosures
in this regard.

children.title: 3. Policy for Empanelment of Independent Valuers
children.text: 3. Policy for Empanelment of Independent Valuers

children.title: 3.1. HFCs should have a procedure for empanelment of professional valuers and
children.text:
3.1. HFCs should have a procedure for empanelment of professional valuers and
maintain a register of 'approved list of valuers'.

children.title: 3.2. HFCs shall prescribe a minimum qualification and minimum post qualification
children.text:
3.2. HFCs shall prescribe a minimum qualification and minimum post qualification
experience for empanelment of valuers. Different qualifications and experience
may be prescribed for different classes of assets (e.g. land and building, plant
and machinery, agricultural land, etc.). While prescribing the qualifications, HFCs
may take into consideration the qualifications prescribed under Section 34AB
(Rule 8A) of the Wealth Tax Act, 1957.

children.title: 3.3. While framing the above policy, HFCs shall also be guided by the provisions of
children.text:
3.3. While framing the above policy, HFCs shall also be guided by the provisions of
the Section 247 of the Companies Act, 2013, Rules made or to be made
thereunder and amendments therein, from time to time. Further, HFCs shall also
be guided by relevant Accounting Standards.
